ctxMarket: Attention is on tokenized assets and institutional-grade products launching on Solana, alongside scrutiny of DeFi security after a Raydium exploit. Solana's support levels are in focus as exchange inflows increase and traders maintain long positions.
ctxTalking:
• Exodus and Ondo launched tokenized trading for over 200 stocks and ETFs on Solana
• Ethena Labs is allocating $250 million to Securitize’s tokenized AAA CLO fund as it deploys on Solana
• A Raydium AMM V3 exploit targeted legacy DeFi contracts, draining $1.34 million
ctxMatters: Tokenized real-world assets and institutional funds are expanding Solana’s use cases beyond crypto-native activity. The Raydium exploit highlights ongoing risks from outdated DeFi infrastructure. Solana’s role as a sponsor for the World Series of Poker signals further mainstream integrations.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Major asset tokenization projects and institutional allocations are moving to Solana; DeFi security risks remain from legacy contracts.
Noise: Speculation about short-term price moves tied to exchange inflows without clear causal evidence.
ctxGlossary:
Tokenized Stocks/ETFs — Traditional equities represented as tokens on a blockchain
CLO Fund — Collateralized loan obligation fund, a structured credit investment product
Raydium AMM — Automated market maker protocol on Solana for decentralized trading

readMore.title: Exodus, Ondo launch tokenized markets with 200-plus stocks and ETFs on Solana
Is Solana’s support strong enough to absorb 1.17 mln in exchange inflows?
The next DeFi drain could come from legacy contracts everyone forgot
